title: Indus
description: Turn Minecraft into an industrial automation game: build machines, expand production lines, manage energy and logistics, and push your factory toward higher tech eras.
body:
Main mod for the Indus Automation modpack. You shouldn't play this mod by itself.

Indus is a fully automated tech-progression mod. It plays like a streamlined GregTech: you place a single controller block and the multiblock builds itself. Every machine hooks into a shared energy network and a three-tier maintenance system that throttles performance if you neglect it.

Features
- Self-building multiblocks
Place a controller and the full structure auto-assembles. No guessing layouts or placing hundreds of blocks by hand.
- Unified energy network
All machines connect to a single power graph that tracks generation, storage, and consumption across your entire base.
- Maintenance system
Machines don’t break, but performance drops sharply if you ignore upkeep. Higher tiers demand real automation.
- Factory-scale balancing
Production lines matter. Inputs, outputs, throughput, and cycle times push you to optimize like a proper automation game.
- Progression designed for modpacks
Tiered recipes, structured tech unlocks, and machine chains built to support long-form progression packs.
- Industrial gameplay focus
No magic, no shortcuts. Each machine exists to fill a role in a scalable factory loop.
